title: alkanna orientalis subsp. integrifolia
intro: Alkanna orientalis subsp. integrifolia, also known as the dyer's bugloss or mountain alkanna, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of alkanna orientalis subsp. integrifolia include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. Alkanna orientalis subsp. integrifolia is often prepared as a tincture or a topical cream, and its leaves and roots are used in herbal preparations, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
